codeReview.summary: The reported handoff does not match the scoped implementation for this feature. The feature description requires a fix that restores the Stop timer assertion by addressing the real regression path, but the cited commit is a later refactor that does not touch the Playwright assertion path or the pollSessionBootstrap stabilization that actually fixed the flake.

issues.description: The handoff claims commit `45e0674` implemented this fix, but that commit is a timer-page refactor plus unrelated docs/research asset changes and does not modify `apps/website/e2e/timer-page.spec.ts` or `apps/website/e2e/fixtures/e2e-auth.ts`, where the actual stabilization lives. The concrete fix for transient workspace-switch session errors is in commit `94f7496`, so this feature does not provide a scoped implementation commit that directly contains the claimed behavior fix.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker marked `frontend-test-worker` as followed even though the skill explicitly says that if the behavior was already fixed by an earlier commit or no scoped implementation was needed, the handoff must say so instead of pointing at an unrelated commit. This review found the actual fix in `94f7496`, while the handoff points at `45e0674`.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/frontend-test-worker/SKILL.md step 10 requires the handoff commitId to directly contain the feature changes; the worker transcript and handoff instead report commit `45e067442aa1cbb68ce0d36c48e29384b9fcdcdb`, while `git show --stat 94f74960a076c6dba6ba112823a8dd4329f07743` is the commit that adds `pollSessionBootstrap` and updates `timer-page.spec.ts`.

summary: Fail: the Stop timer regression appears fixed in the repository, but this feature handoff misattributes the fix to an unrelated refactor commit, so the scoped implementation evidence does not satisfy the feature review requirements.
